description: The UK coalition government faced significant debate regarding the Health and Social Care Act 2012 and its provisions for mental health parity. Critics argued that the structural reorganization of the NHS would prioritize fiscal efficiency over mandatory mental health service funding, leading to a public clash between healthcare unions and the Department of Health.

key_events.description: Royal College of Psychiatrists voices formal opposition to the bill's structure.
Andrew Lansley, Health Secretary, releases white paper defending the efficiency-first model.
Major protest by NHS workers in London highlights potential 'mental health postcode lottery'.

calibration_notes: Calibrates how public opinion shifts when technical policy reform is framed as a threat to vulnerable populations.
